1	# Analogy: Sub-brick A — identifying `Γ(V, pushPullObj F Y_p) ≅ ∏_σ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	subbrickA
8

10	055
11	
12	## Question
13	For the evaluated-at-`V` augmented Čech section complex (`V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i`), identify
14	degreewise and on differentials
15	`Γ(V, pushPullObj F Y_p) ≅ ∏_{σ : Fin(p+1)→I} Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`.
16	(Q1) Does Mathlib give "sections of a module sheaf over a coproduct scheme = product
17	of sections"? (Q2) Does Mathlib identify pullback-of-a-module-sheaf-along-an-open-
18	immersion with restriction, with definitional sections?
19	
20	## Project artifact(s)
21	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean:135` — `pushPullObj F Y = (pushforward Y.hom).obj ((pullback Y.hom).obj F)`.
22	- `…/CechHigherDirectImage.lean:552,561,599` — `coverCechNerveOver` / `…Aug` / `CechNerve`; degree-`p` object is `∐_σ U_σ` with structure map `q_p = Sigma.desc (fun σ ↦ j_σ)`, `j_σ : U_σ = ⋂_k coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ↪ X`.
23	- `…/CechAcyclic.lean:1501,1513` — `sectionCechFaceRestr` / `sectionCech_objD_apply`: the existing product-of-presheaf-sections + alternating-restriction-sum machinery (`sectionCechProductEquiv`).
24	- `…/QcohRestrictBasicOpen.lean:113,248,283` — project ALREADY uses `restrictFunctor`/`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`/`restrict_obj`/`restrictFunctorComp`.
25	
26	## Decisions identified
27	
28	### Decision: Q2 — pullback along an open immersion = restriction (sections)
29	
30	- **Mathlib idiom**: For an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y`, `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor f : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean:319`) is the "better-defeq" model of pullback, with:
31	  - `restrict_obj` (`Sheaf.lean:328`): `Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` — **`rfl`** sections identification.
32	  - `restrict_map` (`Sheaf.lean:331`): the restriction maps are `rfl`-equal to `M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op`.
33	  - `restrictFunctorIsoPullback f : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f` (`Sheaf.lean:371`) — the bridge to the actual `Scheme.Modules.pullback` that `pushPullObj` uses.
34	  - `restrictFunctorComp` (`Sheaf.lean:392`), `restrictFunctorId`, `restrictFunctorCongr` for the bookkeeping.
35	  Why Mathlib chose it: `restrictFunctor` is literally `SheafOfModules.pushforward` along `f.opensFunctor`, so its object sections are `Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` by `rfl` — exactly the defeq the consumer wants — while `pullback` (a sheafification-of-a-left-adjoint) has no such defeq. Mathlib keeps both and ships the iso.
36	- **Project's current path**: `pushPullObj` is built on raw `Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom`. The project ALREADY adopts the `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` idiom in `QcohRestrictBasicOpen.lean:113–114,248,283–286` to convert `pullback`-along-an-open-immersion to `restrict`, with `restrict_obj` for sections.
37	- **Gap**: identical — Mathlib's idiom is the right one and the project already uses it elsewhere.
38	- **Verdict**: PROCEED (reuse the established project pattern: `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `restrict_obj`).
39	
40	### Decision: Q1 — sections of a module sheaf over a coproduct scheme = product
41	
42	- **Mathlib idiom**: only *binary* and only at the structure-sheaf / general-`TopCat.Sheaf` level:
43	  - `Scheme.coprodPresheafObjIso` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Limits.lean:476`): `(X ⨿ Y).presheaf.obj (op U) ≅ X.presheaf.obj (op (inl⁻¹U)) ⨯ Y.presheaf.obj (op (inr⁻¹U))` — **structure sheaf, binary**.
44	  - `TopCat.Sheaf.isProductOfDisjoint` (`Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/SheafCondition/PairwiseIntersections.lean:430`): for disjoint `U ⊓ V = ⊥`, `F(U ⊔ V) ≅ F(U) ⨯ F(V)` — **general `TopCat.Sheaf C X`, binary, same space**.
45	  - `Scheme.sigmaMk` (`Limits.lean:246`): `(Σ i, f i) ≃ₜ ∐ f` — indexed coproduct of scheme *spaces*, but **no section decomposition**.
46	  There is **no indexed (`σ`-family) version, and none for `SheafOfModules`/`X.Modules`**.
47	- **The off-the-shelf escape that shrinks the gap**: `SheafOfModules.evaluation R X` **preserves all limits** (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/Limits.lean:85,108`), and `X.Modules = SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf` `HasLimits` (`Sheaf.lean:50`). So if the degree-`p` object is recognised as a *product in `X.Modules`*, then `Γ(V, ∏_σ N_σ) ≅ ∏_σ Γ(V, N_σ)` is FREE. This turns "sections over a coproduct OPEN" (no API) into "sections of a product MODULE" (off-the-shelf).
48	- **The single irreducible new lemma**: package the coproduct as a product at the module level —
49	  `pushPullObj F Y_p ≅ ∏_σ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)` in `X.Modules`,
50	  equivalently `(q_p)_* (q_p)^* F ≅ ∏_σ (j_σ)_* (j_σ)^* F` for `q_p = Sigma.desc (fun σ ↦ j_σ)`.
51	  This is the disjoint-union/coproduct sheaf decomposition (a module on `∐_σ U_σ` is the product of its restrictions to the components, which ARE disjoint on the coproduct space even though the `U_σ ⊆ X` overlap).
52	- **Cost of divergence**: must be built. Port path: lift the binary `isProductOfDisjoint`/`coprodPresheafObjIso` argument to (i) finite-indexed `σ`, (ii) `SheafOfModules`. The lift to modules goes through `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf` which is **faithful, reflects isos, and preserves limits** (`Sheaf.lean:75–78`) plus `M.presheaf.IsSheaf` (`Sheaf.lean:126`): build a module-linear comparison map, check it is an iso on the underlying `Ab`-presheaf via the sheaf condition over the disjoint component cover, transport back. NOTE the degree-`p` object identification `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj [p] = ∐_σ U_σ` (fibre powers of the open immersions = intersection opens) is its own geometric bookkeeping, separate from this sheaf-theoretic gap.
53	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (one lemma; everything around it is off-the-shelf).
54	
55	### Decision: route the DIFFERENTIAL through the existing `sectionCechProductEquiv` machinery?
56	
57	- **Mathlib/project idiom**: `CechAcyclic.lean` already proves `sectionCech_objD_apply` (`:1513`): read through `sectionCechProductEquiv`, the cosimplicial coface differential IS the alternating sum `∑ᵢ (-1)ⁱ • (presheaf restriction `sectionCechFaceRestr`)`. That is the exact shape `SectionCechModule.dDiff` consumes.
58	- **Recommendation**: YES — express Sub-brick A's degreewise iso so it lands in the shape `∏_{σ} Γ(coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V, F)` and route the differential-match through `sectionCech_objD_apply` rather than rebuilding the alternating-sum bookkeeping. The only genuinely NEW work is the degreewise *object* iso (Q1 gap + Q2 off-the-shelf + evaluation-preserves-products); the differential is reuse.
59	- **Verdict**: DIVERGE? no — ALIGN with the existing project API (reuse `sectionCech_objD_apply`).
60	
61	## Recommendation
62	
63	Decompose Sub-brick A's degreewise iso `Γ(V, pushPullObj F Y_p) ≅ ∏_σ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)` into a

66	1. `pushforward_obj_obj` (`Sheaf.lean:156`, `rfl`): `Γ(V, (q_p)_* N) = Γ(q_p⁻¹V, N)`. **off-the-shelf**
67	2. **[NEW]** `pushPullObj F Y_p ≅ ∏_σ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)` (coproduct→product at module level). Scope a prover lane: port binary `isProductOfDisjoint`/`coprodPresheafObjIso` to indexed + `SheafOfModules` via `toPresheaf` (faithful/reflects-iso/preserves-limits) + `IsSheaf`.
68	3. `evaluation` preserves products (`Sheaf/Limits.lean:85`): `Γ(V, ∏_σ N_σ) ≅ ∏_σ Γ(V, N_σ)`. **off-the-shelf**
69	4. `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` (`Sheaf.lean:371`): `(j_σ)^*F ≅ F.restrict j_σ`. **off-the-shelf, already used in `QcohRestrictBasicOpen.lean`**
70	5. `restrict_obj` (`Sheaf.lean:328`, `rfl`) + `opensRange`/`image_preimage`: `Γ(j_σ⁻¹V, F.restrict j_σ) = Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`. **off-the-shelf**
71	
72	Then chain to the existing `CechAcyclic.lean` machinery: land in `∏_σ Γ(coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V, F)`
73	and reuse `sectionCech_objD_apply` for the differential. Four of five leaves are off-the-shelf

9	/-!
10	# Čech computation of the higher direct images `Rⁱ f_*` (unconditional)
11	
12	This file constructs the higher derived direct images `Rⁱ f_* F` for `i ≥ 1`
13	**without appealing to injective resolutions** in the category of sheaves of
14	modules. The companion `Cohomology/HigherDirectImage.lean` defines `Rⁱ f_*` as a
15	right derived functor, which requires the ambient category of `O_X`-modules to
16	have enough injectives — a property not currently available for sheaves of
17	modules over a sheaf of rings whose value category varies over the site. The
18	Čech approach developed here sidesteps the issue: it computes `Rⁱ f_* F` as the
19	cohomology of an explicit complex built from the pushforwards of `F` over the
20	finite intersections of an affine open cover, producing an **unconditional**
21	construction of `Rⁱ f_*` for quasi-coherent `F` and separated quasi-compact `f`.
22	
23	Throughout, `f : X ⟶ S` is a quasi-compact, separated morphism of schemes (so
24	all finite intersections of an affine open cover of `X` are again affine), and
25	`F : X.Modules` is a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module. A base change of `f` along
26	`g : S' ⟶ S` is recorded by a cartesian square
27	```
28	  X' --g'--> X
29	  |f'        |f
30	  v          v
31	  S' --g---> S
32	```
33	with `F' = (g')^* F` the pullback of `F` to `X'`.
34	
35	The six main declarations are:
36	
37	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve` — the (augmented) Čech nerve of an affine open
38	  cover, an augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules.
39	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechComplex` — the relative Čech complex in `QCoh(S)`, a
40	  cochain complex of `O_S`-modules whose degree-`p` term is the product of the
41	  pushforwards of `F` over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections of the cover.
42	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechAcyclic.affine` — Čech acyclicity on affines: the Čech
43	  complex of a standard cover of an affine scheme has vanishing cohomology in
44	  all positive degrees (Serre vanishing for quasi-coherent sheaves on affines).
45	* `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage` — the cohomology of the
46	  relative Čech complex is canonically isomorphic to `Rⁱ f_* F` wherever the
47	  derived functor is defined.
48	
49	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`.
50

54	(`lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero`).

7476	\begin{lemma}
7477	[A contracting homotopy of the identity makes homology vanish]
7478	  \label{lem:isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero}
7479	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero}
7480	  Let \(\mathcal{A}\) be a preadditive category and \(D^\bullet\) a cochain complex in
7481	  \(\mathcal{A}\). If there is a homotopy \(\mathrm{id}_{D^\bullet} \simeq 0\) between the
7482	  identity endomorphism of \(D^\bullet\) and the zero endomorphism, then for every degree
7483	  \(p\) the homology object \(H^p(D^\bullet)\) is a zero object.
7484	\end{lemma}
7485	\begin{proof}
7486	  The map induced on homology is a homotopy invariant: homotopic chain maps induce equal
7487	  maps on each homology object. Applying this to the homotopy \(\mathrm{id}_{D^\bullet}
7488	  \simeq 0\), the map induced by \(\mathrm{id}_{D^\bullet}\) on \(H^p(D^\bullet)\) equals the
7489	  map induced by \(0\). The former is \(\mathrm{id}_{H^p(D^\bullet)}\) (homology is a
7490	  functor) and the latter is the zero endomorphism of \(H^p(D^\bullet)\); hence
7491	  \(\mathrm{id}_{H^p(D^\bullet)} = 0\). An object whose identity morphism equals its zero
7492	  endomorphism is a zero object, so \(H^p(D^\bullet)\) is zero.
7493	\end{proof}
7494	
7495	\begin{lemma}
7496	[The degree-\(p\) {\v C}ech backbone is the coproduct of intersection opens]
7497	  \label{lem:cech_backbone_left_sigma}
7498	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechBackbone_left_sigma}
7499	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve, def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
7500	  Let \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be an open cover with cover arrow
7501	  \(q : \coprod_{i \in I} U_i \to X\), and let \(Y_p\) denote the degree-\(p\) object of the
7502	  {\v C}ech nerve \(\operatorname{coverCechNerveOver}\mathcal{U}\) of
7503	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_cech_nerve} --- the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power of \(q\) over
7504	  \(X\). Then \(Y_p\) is canonically the coproduct
7505	  \[
7506	    Y_p \;\cong\; \coprod_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I} U_\sigma,
7507	    \qquad
7508	    U_\sigma \;=\; \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,\sigma \;=\;
7509	      \bigcap_{k} U_{\sigma(k)},
7510	  \]
7511	  indexed by the multi-indices \(\sigma\), and the structure map \(q_p : Y_p \to X\) is
7512	  \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}(\sigma \mapsto j_\sigma)\), where
7513	  \(j_\sigma : U_\sigma \hookrightarrow X\) is the open immersion of the intersection open
7514	  \(U_\sigma\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}. In words: the fibre power
7515	  of the open immersions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\) is the intersection open, and the disjoint
7516	  union over \(\sigma\) assembles the {\v C}ech backbone.
7517	\end{lemma}
7518	\begin{proof}
7519	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve, def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
7520	  The coproduct \(\coprod_i U_i\) has, in degree \(p\), the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power over
7521	  \(X\); fibre products distribute over coproducts, so \(\bigl(\coprod_i U_i\bigr)^{\times_X
7522	  (p+1)} \cong \coprod_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I} U_{\sigma(0)} \times_X \cdots
7523	  \times_X U_{\sigma(p)}\). Each factor \(U_{\sigma(k)} \hookrightarrow X\) is an open
7524	  immersion, and the fibre product of open immersions over \(X\) is the intersection of
7525	  their images, so \(U_{\sigma(0)} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{\sigma(p)} \cong \bigcap_k
7526	  U_{\sigma(k)} = U_\sigma\). Under this identification the structure map of the
7527	  \(\sigma\)-component is the open immersion \(j_\sigma\), and the universal map out of the
7528	  coproduct is \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}(\sigma \mapsto j_\sigma)\).
7529	\end{proof}

7531	\begin{lemma}
7532	[Push--pull over the {\v C}ech backbone is the product over intersection opens]
7533	  \label{lem:pushPull_sigma_iso}
7534	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_sigma_iso}
7535	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, lem:cech_backbone_left_sigma, lem:coprodPresheafObjIso_mathlib,
7536	    lem:isProductOfDisjoint_mathlib}
7537	  With the notation of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_backbone_left_sigma}, the push--pull object on
7538	  the degree-\(p\) backbone decomposes as a product over the multi-indices:
7539	  \[
7540	    \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,Y_p
7541	      \;\cong\;
7542	    \prod_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I}
7543	      \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,(\operatorname{Over.mk} j_\sigma)
7544	    \qquad \text{in } X.\mathrm{Modules},
7545	  \]

7547	  (j_\sigma)^*\mathcal{F}\).
7548	\end{lemma}

7551	    lem:isProductOfDisjoint_mathlib}
7552	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_backbone_left_sigma} the backbone \(Y_p\) is the coproduct
7553	  \(\coprod_\sigma U_\sigma\) with structure map \(q_p = \operatorname{Sigma.desc}(\sigma
7554	  \mapsto j_\sigma)\). A sheaf of modules on a coproduct scheme is the product of its
7555	  restrictions to the components: although the images \(U_\sigma \subseteq X\) overlap, the
7556	  components are \emph{disjoint inside the coproduct space} \(\coprod_\sigma U_\sigma\), so
7557	  the sheaf condition over the disjoint cover by the components expresses the sections over
7558	  any open as the product of the sections over the component traces. Pulling \(\mathcal{F}\)
7559	  back along \(q_p\) and pushing forward therefore yields, componentwise, the push--pull of
